message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first get_text inside 2 wezterm poll helpers (tick 281)

Closes 2 maybe-cx seam holes in `frankenterm-core/wezterm.rs` where a
cx-first fn was calling ambient `get_text` despite the trait offering
a cx-first sibling (added in tick 264).

**`PaneWaiter::wait_for_with_cx` poll loop (L3577):**

- `self.source.get_text(pane_id, self.options.escapes)` →
  `get_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, self.options.escapes)`

This fn is the cx-first entry for the main pane-wait polling helper
used by workflow wait conditions and CLI wait-for commands. Before
this tick, the pane-text fetch inside the poll loop did not respect
caller cx — a cancel would still have to complete the pending get_text
RPC before the next checkpoint.

**`wait_for_codex_session_summary_with_cx` poll loop (L3791):**

- `source.get_text(pane_id, options.escapes)` →
  `get_text_with_cx(cx, pane_id, options.escapes)`

Same pattern — the codex session-summary watcher's poll loop was using
ambient get_text despite being in a cx-first fn with a `cx` param in
scope. Now propagates cancel into each poll iteration.

Both legacy ambient `wait_for` and `wait_for_codex_session_summary` are
retained unchanged (legacy path preserved per AGENTS.md contract).

This pattern of "cx-first fn calls ambient inner helper" is the
maybe-cx seam-hole class — fixing them requires only changing the
method name at the call site since the Cx is already in scope.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 95 (187-281).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (runtime 11 + mcp 2 + tui 8 + web 4
+ mcp_helpers 2 + mcp_tools 10 + workflows/runner 1
+ workflows/account_steps 1 + tx_execution 3 + simulation 1
+ workflows/wait_execution 2 + wezterm trait ext 1 + recording 2
+ wezterm poll helpers 2).
main.rs cross-crate total: 173 sites (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
